title: Dissolving and Separating Mixtures Lab
wsObjectives: Use evidence to explain dissolving and recovery

effectTimelines.label: Particles dissolve
effectTimelines.description: Granules shrink and disperse in the beaker.

effectTimelines.label: Residue remains
effectTimelines.description: Undissolved solids settle at the bottom.

steps.title: Frame the investigation
steps.prompt: How does changing water temperature affect how quickly a substance dissolves?
steps.guidance: Pick the investigation question that best matches the test you want to run.

options.label: How does temperature affect dissolving speed?
How does stirring affect dissolving speed?

steps.title: Plan variables and controls
steps.prompt: Choose what you will change and what you will keep the same.
steps.guidance: A strong plan identifies the independent variable and at least two control variables.

options.label: Keep the mass and substance the same

steps.title: Predict using variables
steps.prompt: Now that you know the variables, predict which condition will dissolve the solid fastest and explain why.
steps.guidance: Link your prediction to particle movement and energy.
steps.sentenceStem: I predict ___ will dissolve fastest because ___.

steps.title: Run the dissolve test
steps.prompt: Select a substance, water temperature and stirring level, then run the visual experiment.
steps.guidance: Watch the particles carefully before you record the result.

steps.title: Record observations
steps.prompt: Record the dissolve time, or note that the solid settled again without dissolving.
steps.guidance: Use the scene and the trial summary as evidence.

steps.title: Analyse patterns
steps.prompt: What pattern do you see between particle behaviour and dissolving speed?
steps.guidance: Compare trials rather than describing only one result.

steps.title: Conclude with evidence
steps.prompt: Answer the original question using trial evidence.
steps.guidance: Quote at least one measured time and explain whether the solid dissolved or settled.

steps.title: Evaluate the method
steps.prompt: How could this dissolving test be made more reliable?
steps.guidance: Think about repeats, timing accuracy and control variables.

teacherNotes: Use the procedural scene to discuss particle models as simplifications rather than exact reality.
Ask pupils to justify whether a residue counts as evidence that a substance has not dissolved.
safetyNote: Remind pupils that the scene models hot water safely, but real hot water investigations require adult supervision and heat-safe equipment.
misconceptionChecks: Dissolving is not the same as disappearing completely.
A substance can mix into a solution and still be present.

hero.blurb: Watch solids break apart in a procedural beaker scene and compare how conditions change dissolving time.
